Task id: T81 (ledgerRefs goals:G4)
Base commit: a4ad01d (main)
Branch: implement/T81 — your worktree is created by native isolation; FIRST run `git switch -c implement/T81` inside YOUR worktree so your commit lands on the task branch the orchestrator merges.

NOTE: this is a Go repository (wanbond). The check gate is `go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...` via `nix develop -c` if go is not on PATH (per AGENTS.md); there is no bun here.

headline: Validate per-peer psk presence, distinctness, and single-peer back-compat

description: Extend config.validate: when len(peers) > 1, require each peer's `psk` present and pairwise-distinct (equal psks defeat authenticated demux) and each peer `name` present and unique; when len(peers) == 1, accept top-level psk as the default and require no per-peer psk. Reject a per-peer psk that duplicates another peer's. ALSO reject an EDGE role config with >1 peer (concentrator-only scope, Q21 — the edge dials exactly one concentrator peer per process). Keep the rest of edge validation unchanged. Fail fast with precise messages.

acceptance: Table-driven config.validate test: >1 peer with equal per-peer psks fails; >1 peer with a missing per-peer psk fails; duplicate peer names fail; edge role with 2 peers fails with a scope-explaining message; single-peer top-level-only passes; 2 peers with distinct psks+names pass. `go test ./internal/config/...` passes.

- baboon: Baboon data model compiler reference. Covers model syntax, multi-version evolution, code generation, wire-format conversions, database migrations, backward compatibility, and version negotiation. TRIGGER when: designing, changing, or discussing any external API, working with .baboon model files, updating data models, adding new fields or types, bumping model versions, running code generation, implementing conversions, writing database migrations, or discussing API versioning strategy.
- constructive-test-taxonomy: Multi-axis classification of tests (Intention × Encapsulation × Isolation) for evaluating test quality and steering test-writing decisions. Replaces fuzzy Unit/Integration/E2E vocabulary with precise tags and a maintenance-cost heuristic. TRIGGER when: writing new tests, reviewing a test suite, deciding what to test or at what level, discussing mocks vs. real dependencies, classifying tests, or arguing about test coverage or test-strategy tradeoffs.
- dual-tests: Pattern for testing code that touches external systems: write one abstract test suite against an interface, then run it against both the production adapter (real DB/API/FS) and a hand-written in-memory dummy. Prefers manual dummies over auto-generated mocks. TRIGGER when: designing code that depends on a database, HTTP API, message queue, filesystem, or other external system; choosing between mocks, fakes, or dummies; adding integration points; setting up test infrastructure; or when the `constructive-test-taxonomy` skill calls for moving Communication-tier tests toward Blackbox-Atomic.

TRIGGER only when $SMIND_SANDBOXED=1 — i.e. running inside the yolo sandbox and you need to reach something outside it. SKIP entirely when $SMIND_SANDBOXED is unset: you are unsandboxed, so write directly and ignore this skill.

Writes NOTHING to the ledger — the orchestrator records the terminal review.

First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

421		// uplink in bits/s, parsed from LinkBandwidthRaw in normalize. It is used ONLY to
422		// size the weighted scheduler's per-path pace from the bandwidth-delay product
423		// (SizePacingFromBDP) when the weighted policy runs with pacing ENABLED (T53, Q20);

441	// WireGuard holds the inner tunnel's key material.
442	type WireGuard struct {
443		PrivateKey Key    `toml:"private_key"`
444		Peers      []Peer `toml:"peers"`
445		// ListenPort is the UDP port the concentrator listens on; 0 on the edge.
446		ListenPort uint16 `toml:"listen_port"`
447	}
448	
449	// Peer is one WireGuard peer.
450	type Peer struct {
451		PublicKey Key `toml:"public_key"`
452		// Endpoint is the peer's SINGLE tunnel address (edge -> concentrator); the
453		// legacy single-endpoint config form, mutually exclusive with EndpointsRaw.
454		// Empty on the concentrator, which roams the edge's endpoint dynamically.
455		// A bare `endpoint = "..."` is normalized in resolveEndpoints to a one-
456		// element Endpoints list, so it stays behavior-identical to the pre-T54
457		// config surface.
458		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint"`
459		// EndpointsRaw is the ORDERED list of concentrator (peer) endpoint address:port
460		// strings for the edge-side hub-failover config surface (Q18): index 0 is the
461		// active/primary concentrator, the rest are ordered standbys. Edge-only; a
462		// concentrator declaring it is a config error (validate). Mutually exclusive
463		// with the legacy Endpoint field.
464		EndpointsRaw []string `toml:"endpoints"`
465		// Endpoints is EndpointsRaw (or the single legacy Endpoint, as a one-element
466		// list) parsed to netip.AddrPort and de-duplicated; populated by
467		// resolveEndpoints in normalize(). This is the shape T57's hub-failover
468		// switch consumes: Endpoints[0] is the active concentrator, Endpoints[1:]
469		// are the ordered standbys to fail over to in order when all paths to the
470		// active one are down.
471		Endpoints []netip.AddrPort `toml:"-"`
472		// AllowedIPs are the CIDR ranges routed to this peer.
473		AllowedIPs []string `toml:"allowed_ips"`
474	}
475	
476	// resolveEndpoints canonicalizes the peer's endpoint config into the ordered
477	// Endpoints list (Q18): the legacy single Endpoint field and the new ordered
478	// EndpointsRaw list are mutually exclusive input forms, but a bare `endpoint =
479	// "..."` is normalized here to a ONE-ELEMENT Endpoints list — so it stays
480	// behavior-identical to the pre-T54 single-defaultRemote config surface. Each
481	// entry must parse as netip.AddrPort ("host:port"), the same format
482	// bind.Multipath.ParseEndpoint requires of the UAPI endpoint string at
483	// runtime, and duplicates within the list are rejected.
484	func (p *Peer) resolveEndpoints() error {
485		if p.Endpoint != "" && len(p.EndpointsRaw) > 0 {
486			return errors.New("endpoint and endpoints are mutually exclusive; endpoint is the single-entry legacy form of endpoints")
487		}
488		raw := p.EndpointsRaw
489		if p.Endpoint != "" {
490			raw = []string{p.Endpoint}
491		}
492		seen := make(map[netip.AddrPort]struct{}, len(raw))
493		endpoints := make([]netip.AddrPort, 0, len(raw))
494		for _, s := range raw {
495			ap, err := netip.ParseAddrPort(s)
496			if err != nil {
497				return fmt.Errorf("invalid endpoint %q: %w", s, err)
498			}
499			if _, dup := seen[ap]; dup {
500				return fmt.Errorf("duplicate endpoint %q", s)
501			}
502			seen[ap] = struct{}{}
503			endpoints = append(endpoints, ap)
504		}
505		p.Endpoints = endpoints
506		return nil
507	}

package config

import (
	"encoding/base64"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

// testKey returns a syntactically valid base64 32-byte key for fixtures.
func testKey(b byte) string {
	raw := make([]byte, keyLen)
	for i := range raw {
		raw[i] = b
	}
	return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(raw)
}

// writeConfig writes body to a temp file with the given mode and returns its path.
func writeConfig(t *testing.T, mode os.FileMode, body string) string {
	t.Helper()
	dir := t.TempDir()
	path := filepath.Join(dir, "wanbond.toml")
	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(body), mode); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("write fixture: %v", err)
	}
	// os.WriteFile is subject to umask; force the exact bits.
	if err := os.Chmod(path, mode); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("chmod fixture: %v", err)
	}
	return path
}

// Root-level scalars (role, psk) must precede any TOML table header.
const edgeConfig = `
role = "edge"
psk = "%PSK%"

[[paths]]
name = "starlink"
source_addr = "192.0.2.10"

[[paths]]
name = "cellular"
source_addr = "192.0.2.20"

[wireguard]
private_key = "%PRIV%"
[[wireguard.peers]]
public_key = "%PUB%"
endpoint = "203.0.113.5:51820"
allowed_ips = ["0.0.0.0/0"]

[amnezia]
jc = 4
jmin = 8
jmax = 80
s1 = 15
s2 = 92

[metrics]
listen = "127.0.0.1:9095"

[log]
level = "info"
`

const concentratorConfig = `
role = "concentrator"
psk = "%PSK%"

[[paths]]
name = "wan"
source_addr = "203.0.113.5"

[wireguard]
private_key = "%PRIV%"
listen_port = 51820
[[wireguard.peers]]

    config: add per-peer psk and name fields to config.Peer (T80)
    
    WireGuard.Peer gains optional PSK (toml: psk) and Name (toml: name) fields
    for the G4 multi-peer concentrator groundwork. Top-level Config.PSK stays
    the single-peer default: nothing normalizes/backfills a per-peer PSK from
    it, so a legacy single-peer config carrying only the top-level psk parses
    to a byte-identical Config (golden struct compare). No datapath change;
    the new fields are parsed and exposed only.

    TestLoadSinglePeerLegacyPSKGoldenShape (legacy single-peer TOML, golden
    reflect.DeepEqual compare against a hand-built expected Config).

464		// with the legacy Endpoint field.

468		// resolveEndpoints in normalize(). This is the shape T57's hub-failover

470		// are the ordered standbys to fail over to in order when all paths to the
471		// active one are down. A hostname entry (Q35) contributes NO element here at
472		// load time — resolution happens at runtime, not at config load (Q30) — so
473		// Endpoints only ever holds the literal-address entries, in order.
474		Endpoints []netip.AddrPort `toml:"-"`

477		// error naming this flag. Default-off, so an existing IP-literal config never
478		// takes a new code path. Edge-only; a concentrator declaring dns = true is a
479		// config error, mirroring the endpoints-not-meaningful-for-concentrator rule.
480		DNS bool `toml:"dns"`
481		// EndpointSpecs is the ordered, typed per-entry parse of the endpoint/
482		// endpoints entries (Q35): each entry is either a literal address:port
483		// (IsName=false, Addr set — parsed with EXACTLY today's netip.ParseAddrPort
484		// path) or, when DNS opts in, a hostname:port (IsName=true, Host/Port set).
485		// Populated by resolveEndpoints in normalize(); order matches the TOML list.
486		EndpointSpecs []EndpointSpec `toml:"-"`
487		// AllowedIPs are the CIDR ranges routed to this peer.

504	// entry parses via netip.ParseAddrPort and sets Addr (IsName=false); a
505	// hostname:port entry (accepted only when the owning Peer opts in with

507	// config load (Q30 defers it to runtime).
508	type EndpointSpec struct {
509		// Host is the hostname, set only when IsName is true.
510		Host string
511		// Port is the port for a hostname entry, set only when IsName is true.
512		Port uint16
513		// Addr is the parsed literal address:port, set only when IsName is false.

516		// (false).

521	// Endpoints list (Q18) and the ordered, typed EndpointSpecs list (Q35): the

553				}
554				seen[ap] = struct{}{}
555				endpoints = append(endpoints, ap)
556				specs = append(specs, EndpointSpec{Addr: ap})
557				continue
558			}
559			host, portStr, splitErr := net.SplitHostPort(s)
560			if splitErr != nil {

565			// error rather than diverting it into the hostname path, so every

# Agent instructions — wanbond

Instructions for AI agents (and humans) working in this repo. Read the
[README](README.md) for what wanbond is and [docs/design.md](docs/design.md) for
the architecture before changing anything.

## Keep the docs current (required)

**Documentation is part of the definition of done.** When a change alters
behavior, configuration, architecture, the wire format, an invariant, or an
operational step, update the affected docs **in the same change**:

- **[README.md](README.md)** — the front door: capabilities, quick start, repo
  layout, status/limitations. Update when a capability, config surface, package,
  or limitation changes.
- **[docs/design.md](docs/design.md)** — architecture and what we built on top of
  amneziawg-go. Update when the data path, a layer/package, an invariant, the
  security model, or a deliberate boundary changes.
- **[docs/install.md](docs/install.md)** — setup and operation. Update when
  config keys, systemd units, firewall/provisioning steps, or metrics change.
- **[docs/manual-checklist.md](docs/manual-checklist.md)** — update when a
  verification step or a real-link measurement changes.

A change that moves the code but not the docs it invalidates is **incomplete**.
If you are unsure whether a doc is affected, grep it for the symbol/keyword you
touched. Prefer editing the existing doc over adding a new one; keep docs
consistent with each other (e.g. a config key described in README, design, and
install must match).

## Build, test, lint

The dev shell (`nix develop`) puts Go 1.26, golangci-lint, and the netem/DPI
tooling on `PATH`. Run tools from it (e.g. `nix develop --command bash -c '…'`).

```sh
just build      # go build ./...
just test       # unprivileged unit/property tests (go test ./...)
just lint       # go vet + golangci-lint, INCLUDING -tags e2e and -tags realhosts
just e2e        # privileged netns fixture: sudo -E go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...
just realhosts  # opt-in real-machine tier (-tags realhosts), report-only

config: validate per-peer psk presence/distinctness + single-peer back-compat (T81)

Extend config.validate for the Q21 multi-peer concentrator surface (T80 added
the Peer.PSK/Peer.Name fields; this task wires the invariants):

- len(peers) > 1: each peer's psk is required and must be pairwise-distinct
  (equal psks defeat authenticated demux); each peer's name is required and
  must be unique.
- len(peers) == 1: the top-level psk remains the default; a per-peer psk is
  rejected as redundant.

  explaining message (Q21 — the edge dials exactly one concentrator peer per
  process); the rest of edge validation is unchanged.

six cases (equal psks, missing psk, duplicate names, edge with 2 peers,
single-peer top-level-only pass, 2-peer distinct pass).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
